### Post by towanda on 2006-11-16
I am running Dapper 6.06 64-bit on a Gateway MX6448 laptop.  I edited .xorg/conf and got the touchpad to behave better, but it still copies and pastes text and commands on its own, and still clicks by itself if it hovers over a button for too long.  How do I fix that?  Here is my .xorg/conf:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizScrollDelta"	"0"
	Option		"MaxMapTime"		"50"
	Options		"SHMConfig"		"1"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "stylus"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "stylus"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "eraser"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "eraser"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
  Driver        "wacom"
  Identifier    "cursor"
  Option        "Device"        "/dev/wacom"          # Change to 
                                                      # /dev/input/event
                                                      # for USB
  Option        "Type"          "cursor"
  Option        "ForceDevice"   "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS482)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:5:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon Xpress 200M (RS482)"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection

		Modes		"1280x800"

		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x800"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

### Post by towanda on 2006-11-18
Well, I have a partial fix.  I made my /etc/X11/xorg.conf read thusly:

Section "InputDevice"
Identifier "Synaptics Touchpad"
Driver "synaptics"
Option "SendCoreEvents" "true"
Option "SHMConfig" "no"          <-- placed this line here and set it to "no"
Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
Option "Protocol" "auto-dev"
Option "HorizScrollDelta" "0"
Option "MaxMapTime" "0"          <-- changed this back to 0.
EndSection

I also made the following change in Firefox:

In the location bar, I typed:

about:config

Then I searched for 

mousewheel.horizscroll.withnokey.action

Double-clicked the line and set the value to 1 instead of 2.

This has helped some, but the touchpad still copies and pastes at random and, I just now discovered, automatically grabs any scroll bar it happens to roll over.  It also brings into focus whatever window the cursor happens to be in.  If anyone can help with these remaining issues I would greatly appreciate it.

---

### Post by aseneca on 2006-11-18
this is whats in mine for the touchpad

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ExplorerPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"MaxTapTime"		"0"
EndSection

no problems

alternative you can use the package qsynaptics.... to graphically manage

AS

---

### Post by towanda on 2006-11-19
Interesting.  Do you have only one "InputDevice" section for your mouse?  My xorg.conf has two -- one for Configured Mouse and one for Synaptics Touchpad.  I tried using qsynaptics, but many of the options are grayed out and unselectable (in general I prefer using the command line anyway).  Any further advice would be appreciated.

---

### Post by towanda on 2006-11-19
**aseneca**, I edited my first InputDevice section to match yours, and wound up with the original problems back.  Then I edited it so that the Configured Driver is "mouse" and moved the "MaxTapTime" line from the second InputDevice section to the first one.  That fixed everything but the apparently random copy-and-paste problem.

---

### Post by towanda on 2006-11-23
Nobody knows how to fix the random copy-and-paste issue?

### Post by amp_man on 2006-11-24
HP Pavilion ze2000 here, with a synaptics touchpad also, and I've never had that issue. I think you might have a hardware issue, try setting "Emulate3buttons" to "false" and see if that fixes your problem, i think your touchpad might think, for some random reason, that both buttons are being pressed when they're not. Also, thanks for all the info you've posted, I haven't had some of your problems, but my touchpad likes to click when I don't want it to also, so your experience should be helpful.

---
